name: Caffeine
summary: Decision-ready summary: Caffeine is best framed for alertness; reaction time; fatigue reduction; safety screen: anxiety disorder; uncontrolled hypertension; arrhythmia.
description: Caffeine supports energy; focus; performance with high confidence in this merged decision layer.
safetyNotes: Avoid high doses with anxiety, hypertension, arrhythmia, pregnancy caffeine limits, or stimulant sensitivity.

sources.title: PubMed PMID 20182035. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.citation: PubMed PMID 20182035. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.note: evidence citation lookup; PMID source present in Evidence_Register

contraindications: cardiac arrhythmia or a recent heart attack warrants clinician guidance due to increased risk of palpitations and abnormal heart rhythm
anxiety disorders or panic disorder due to caffeine's stimulant effect worsening anxiety and triggering panic symptoms
pregnancy or breastfeeding warrants limiting intake to under 200 mg per day due to an association between higher intake and low birth weight or pregnancy loss
MAOI antidepressants due to a risk of rapid heart rate and elevated blood pressure
stimulant medications such as amphetamines or ADHD medications due to additive effects on heart rate and blood pressure
children without pediatrician guidance due to a lack of established safe dosing.
